key_insight: An autonomous LLM coding agent improved a crystal graph neural network for band-gap prediction and achieved the best no-pretraining result on MatBench by combining known modeling ideas.
engineering_relevance: This suggests LLM agents can accelerate materials-model iteration for property prediction, especially on standardized benchmarks with clear metrics and strong baselines.
method: Started from an expert-designed crystal graph network for band-gap prediction.
Added element-pair features on message-passing edges.
Added a crystal space-group embedding, drawing on methods already used in crystal ML and broader ML.
evidence_signal: On the MatBench band-gap benchmark with more than 100k crystals, the agent-built model outperformed all 17 expert-designed models reported for the task without external pretraining.
watch_item: The gain came from integrating established techniques rather than discovering new physics, so autonomous success may depend more on recombining known ideas than on true novelty.
paper_title: Optimizing Expert-Designed Crystal Graph Networks for Band-Gap Prediction with an Autonomous LLM Research Loop
